rationale: The weakness report shows the trained model spamming unproductive MARITIME_TRADE actions (e.g. repeatedly dumping WOOD->ORE) — classic churn that also gives away scarce resources without enabling any build. The original maritime grader penalized churn only mildly (-w['churn']) and applied scarcity linearly, so the policy could keep over-trading at low cost. The improved grader keeps the exact same gt schema (legal_actions / trade_options / weights / no_trade_reward) but widens the gap: enabling trades get an extra productivity bonus, churn (no enable, no progress) is penalized at roughly double weight, and giving scarce resources is penalized 1.5x. This sharply separates a productive trade that unlocks a settlement/city from a pointless resource swap, steering the model away from its observed over-trading failure mode while leaving all existing scenarios valid.

sanity.good_answer: After analysis, trading ore for brick lets me build a settlement. <answer>1</answer>
sanity.garbage_answer: hmm i really am not sure what to do here
